description: There is no standalone cross3d() function. To compute a cross product of two 3D vectors, use trace3d with a vector operation or compute it manually. The cross product produces a vector perpendicular to both inputs following the right-hand rule.
utility: Cross product of two 3D vectors (no standalone function -- use vector operations)

alternates.syntax: vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, ay*bz - az*by, az*bx - ax*bz, ax*by - ay*bx)
alternates.description: Compute cross product manually from known component values

examples.scenario: Cross product of x-axis and y-axis unit vectors gives z-axis
examples.expression:
G = g3d(at(4,23), view(30, 30))
i = vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, c(red))
j = vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, c(green))
k = vector3d(G, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1, c(blue))

examples.scenario: Normal vector to two direction vectors computed manually
